title: mentha viridis subsp. inarimensis
intro: Mentha viridis subsp. inarimensis, also known as Japanese mint, is a subspecies of mint native to Japan. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Japanese mint include its ability to alleviate nausea, reduce fever, and soothe digestive discomfort. Japanese mint can be prepared as a tea, added to salads, or used in herbal infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
